Key Finding: TSHZ3 is a candidate gene with significant co-occurring mutations in the Pan-Cancer set whose carcinogenic role remains uncharacterized.
Key Finding Citation: A large number of pairs (142) with significant co-occurring mutations is identified only considering the Pan-Cancer data set ( Extended Data Fig. 4 ); these pairs include several candidate genes (for example, NAV3 , RPL22 and TSHZ3 ) whose function in carcinogenesis is not well characterized.

Key Finding: VHL mutations are a signature exclusive to kidney renal clear cell carcinoma (52%) and serve as a common driver in KIRC clusters.
Key Finding Citation: Signature mutations exclusive to KIRC include those affecting VHL (52%) and PBRM1 (33%) ( Fig. 2 ).

Key Finding: WT1 is part of a set of genes showing strong mutual exclusivity in a Pan-Cancer analysis of 600 non-cancer-type-specific genes.
Key Finding Citation: When 600 non-cancer-type-specific genes were added to the analysis (Methods), we identified another set consisting of TP53 , PIK3CA , PIK3R1 , SETD2 and WT1 ( P < 0.01; Extended Data Fig. 5b and Supplementary Table 7 ).
